name: Story Builder
description: Create and manage fictional worlds with full CRUD across 9 dimensions: characters, relationships, arcs, factions, lore, rules, settings, themes, and scenes. All lookups by name — no UUIDs needed. AI-powered generation uses full world context.
author: ultralight

create_world.description: Create a new fictional world. Returns a world_id used by all other tools.

add.description: Batch-create any combination of entities in a single call. Characters, settings, themes, relationships, arcs, factions, lore, rules, and scenes. Relationships reference characters by name. Create characters and their relationships in the same call.

characters.description: Characters to create. Each: { name, role?, traits?: string[], backstory? }

settings.description: Locations to create. Each: { name, description }

themes.description: Themes to create. Each: { name, description }

relationships.description: Relationships to create. Each: { character_a, character_b, type, description? }. Use character names, not IDs.

arcs.description: Narrative arcs. Each: { name, type?: 'narrative'|'character'|'political'|'timeline', description, season?, episode_range?, characters?: string[] (names) }

factions.description: Factions/groups. Each: { name, description, members?: string[] (character names) }

lore.description: Lore entries. Each: { name, type?: 'institution'|'media'|'artifact'|'event'|'cultural'|'technology', description }

rules.description: World rules/constraints. Each: { name, type?: 'constraint'|'tone'|'accuracy'|'structural', description }

scenes.description: Scenes to add. Each: { title, content, type?: 'scene'|'chapter'|'dialogue', character_names?: string[], setting_name? }

returns.description: { created: { characters: [{id, name}], relationships: [{id, between, type}], ... } }

read.description: Read world data. Returns all dimensions by default, or filter with 'include'. Zoom into a single character with 'character_name' to see their profile, relationships, scenes, and faction memberships. Paginate scenes with scene_limit/scene_offset.

character_name.description: Zoom into one character — returns their full profile, relationships, scenes they appear in, and factions

returns.description: Full world data with all requested dimensions

update.description: Batch-update entities by name or ID. Partial updates — only specified fields change. For characters: merge_traits=true appends traits instead of replacing. For factions: use add_members/remove_members to modify membership. For relationships: use 'between' with [charA_name, charB_name].

world.description: Update world metadata: { name?, genre?, description? }

characters.description: Each: { name_or_id, name?, role?, traits?, backstory?, merge_traits?: bool }

arcs.description: Each: { name_or_id, name?, type?, description?, season?, episode_range?, characters?: string[] }

factions.description: Each: { name_or_id, name?, description?, add_members?: string[], remove_members?: string[] }

remove.description: Batch-delete entities by name or ID. Deleting a character also removes their relationships. Delete relationships by name pair ('Cash Bo / Blazer Sith') or by ID.

characters.description: Character names or IDs to delete (also removes their relationships)

settings.description: Setting names or IDs to delete

themes.description: Theme names or IDs to delete

relationships.description: Relationship IDs or name pairs ('Name A / Name B') to delete

arcs.description: Arc names or IDs to delete

factions.description: Faction names or IDs to delete

lore.description: Lore names or IDs to delete

rules.description: Rule names or IDs to delete

scenes.description: Scene titles or IDs to delete

get_context.description: Full world dump optimized for agent handoff. 'structured' returns all data as JSON. 'narrative' uses AI to generate a concise story bible brief — covers premise, characters, dynamics, political landscape, active arcs, rules, and timeline. Best way for a new agent to pick up where the last one left off.

returns.description: Full world state in requested format

generate.description: AI-powered content generation using full world context (all 9 dimensions). Automatically saves as a scene in the timeline. Reference characters and settings by name.

prompt.description: Creative direction (e.g. 'Cash Bo confronts The Earl about the BBF ratings')

character_names.description: Focus characters by name — their relationships are highlighted in context

save.description: Save generated content as a scene (default: true)

returns.description: { type, content, scene_id, saved, world, scene_order }
